use_cases.title: Molecular diagnosis of T cell-mediated rejection
use_cases.clinical_question: Can public liver graft omics data identify molecular evidence that separates TCMR from no rejection?
use_cases.why_it_matters: Histology-based rejection diagnosis can be ambiguous. A molecular evidence layer may help interpret borderline biopsies and prioritize validation markers.
current_evidence: GSE145780 provides graft-liver bulk transcriptome evidence for TCMR versus no_rejection; full-platform gene-level contrasts are generated when differential_expression.json is present.
CXCL9, CXCL10, IFNG, and GZMB remain pre-specified rejection markers, but they are now treated as marker evidence rather than proof of diagnostic performance.
next_validation: Validate the TCMR contrast in an independent liver transplant cohort.
Add single-cell localization for marker-producing cell types.
Validate in independent bulk and single-cell liver transplant datasets.

use_cases.title: Separate early graft injury from rejection
use_cases.clinical_question: Can molecular signatures distinguish early injury from TCMR when both may present with abnormal graft function?
use_cases.why_it_matters: Early graft dysfunction may reflect ischemia/reperfusion injury, preservation damage, infection, drug toxicity, or rejection. Misclassification can alter immunosuppression decisions.
current_evidence: GSE145780 includes no_rejection, TCMR, early_injury, and fibrosis molecular states.

AGING_2020_LT_SERUM_PROTEOMICS adds direct serum proteomic biomarker evidence separating stable post-transplant recipients from acute rejection and ischemic-type biliary lesion contexts through ACLY and FGA peak-linked protein features.
This use case still requires stronger independent tissue-level injury-versus-rejection cohorts before it should be presented as a resolved classifier.

IJMS_2022_LT_GRAFT_AKI_PROTEOMICS adds postreperfusion graft proteomics from a direct transplant tissue cohort, highlighting early AKI-linked inflammatory and endothelial proteins such as SAA2, PLA2G2A, PECAM1, and APOA1.
next_validation: Add hypoxia, hepatocyte stress, and metabolic injury signatures.
Compare early_injury with TCMR using differential expression, pathway enrichment, and metabolome/proteome evidence where public data exist.
Validate whether serum ACLY and FGA shifts track biopsy-defined injury versus rejection in an independent cohort.
Compare graft-injury proteins such as SAA2, PLA2G2A, PECAM1, and APOA1 against TCMR-oriented transcriptomic markers to avoid conflating injury-context proteomics with rejection-specific evidence.

use_cases.title: Fibrosis and chronic graft injury biology
use_cases.clinical_question: Which extracellular matrix and stromal signals track graft fibrosis or late chronic injury?
use_cases.why_it_matters: Fibrosis is a major determinant of long-term graft function. A molecular view can help identify mechanism and candidate monitoring markers.
current_evidence: GSE145780 provides a small fibrosis group for graft-liver bulk transcriptome exploration.
COL1A1 is marker evidence only; fibrosis should be upgraded to an ECM pathway/module after full-platform contrasts are generated.
next_validation: Expand ECM marker set with COL3A1, ACTA2, TAGLN, LUM, DCN, and TGFBI where platform mappings are available.
Use single-cell, proteome, and pathology-linked data to identify stromal, endothelial, and myeloid contributors.

use_cases.title: Molecular donor liver quality assessment
use_cases.clinical_question: Can donor liver transcriptomics identify molecular features associated with accepted versus rejected donor organs?
use_cases.why_it_matters: Better molecular context may help interpret marginal donors and organ utilization decisions.
current_evidence: GSE243887 provides 32 deceased donor liver RNA-seq biopsies with accepted versus rejected transplant-selection labels.
Raw count evidence is normalized as log2(CPM + 1) and exposed as gene-level summaries plus accepted_donor_liver versus rejected_donor_liver Welch-test contrasts.
ALB, CYP3A4, APOA1, HMOX1, and COL1A1 can now be queried as donor-liver quality marker evidence.
next_validation: Add hepatocyte function, stress response, and fibrosis module scores for donor-quality interpretation.
Link expression evidence to histopathology metadata where reusable public tables are available.
Treat accepted/rejected labels as donor organ selection status, not post-transplant graft outcome.

use_cases.title: Blood-based non-invasive graft monitoring
use_cases.clinical_question: Can blood or PBMC molecular signatures reflect liver graft rejection or injury without relying on repeated biopsies?
use_cases.why_it_matters: A blood-based evidence layer could support longitudinal monitoring and decide when biopsy is warranted.
current_evidence: GSE200340 provides 185 pediatric liver transplant whole-blood/PBMC RNA-seq samples across pre-transplant, early post-transplant, and late post-transplant time points.
RSEM expected_count evidence is normalized as log2(CPM + 1) and exposed as gene-level summaries plus early/late post-transplant versus pre-transplant contrasts.
CXCL10, ISG15, IFI27, and IL7R can now be queried as longitudinal blood monitoring markers.

MDPI_METABO_2024_LT_GRAFT_PATHOLOGY adds 55 post-transplant serum metabolomics samples spanning TCMR, biliary complications, and post-transplant MASH with 134 absolute-concentration metabolite features.

AGING_2020_LT_SERUM_PROTEOMICS adds direct liver-transplant serum proteomics biomarker evidence for ACLY, FGA, and APOA1 across perioperative, acute rejection, and ITBL contexts, with Welch contrasts derived from published group summary statistics.

PXD062924 adds direct post-transplant serum proteomics evidence for normal versus impaired kidney function after liver transplantation, exposing 45 differential proteins with PRIDE-linked provenance and highlighting markers such as B2M, AMBP, TF, APOA1, and SLPI.
next_validation: Recover or curate rejection outcome labels from publication supplementary material if publicly reusable.
Compare blood interferon and T cell signatures with liver graft biopsy signatures.
Treat the current layer as time-point monitoring evidence, not a rejection-outcome classifier.
Validate serum metabolite shifts in an independent graft pathology cohort before claiming non-invasive classification performance.
Keep the serum proteomics layer framed as published group-summary biomarker evidence until a reusable per-sample transplant proteome matrix is public.

Keep the PXD062924 proteomics layer framed as post-transplant renal-dysfunction monitoring evidence rather than rejection-specific classification, and upgrade it only if a reusable sample-level protein matrix becomes public.

use_cases.title: Single-cell mechanism of rejection
use_cases.clinical_question: Which cell types produce rejection-associated markers and which immune compartments expand during graft rejection?
use_cases.why_it_matters: Bulk molecular signals need cellular interpretation before they can guide mechanism, biomarkers, or therapeutic hypotheses.
current_evidence: GSE189539 provides a public filtered single-cell graft-liver count matrix with 58,243 cells from before-LT cold perfusion and after-LT portal reperfusion samples.
V1 exposes queryable marker evidence for EAD-associated immune niche genes including S100A12, GZMB, GZMK, NKG7, and related marker programs.
The GEO matrix does not expose a reusable cell-to-sample or cell-type metadata table, so this layer is marker-level evidence rather than a full cell-composition contrast.
next_validation: Recover public cell metadata or author-processed annotations from Figshare/HCA before claiming cell-type proportions.
Annotate CD8_TRM, myeloid, endothelial, B cell, and parenchymal compartments.
Link cell-type marker expression back to bulk rejection signatures and independent rejection single-cell cohorts.

use_cases.title: Gut-liver axis after liver transplantation
use_cases.clinical_question: Can microbiome features contextualize infection risk, immune activation, rejection-like inflammation, or immunosuppression exposure after liver transplantation?
use_cases.why_it_matters: Post-transplant infection and immune dysregulation are clinically entangled. Microbiome evidence could add a non-host layer that transcriptomics alone cannot answer.
current_evidence: DFI_MICROBIOME_LT_2024 is processed as a direct liver-transplant stool metabolomics and microbiome source for postoperative infection risk.
The current feature layer exposes 156 metabolite records and 769 MetaPhlAn taxon records with unadjusted infection-positive versus infection-negative summaries.
Example searchable features include Enterococcus faecium and bile-acid metabolites such as 12-oxochenodeoxycholic acid.
next_validation: Normalize metabolite names to HMDB/ChEBI/PubChem and taxon names to NCBI taxonomy identifiers.
Reproduce the publication's multivariable infection-risk models before claiming predictive performance.
Search MGnify/ENA/SRA for additional liver transplantation stool, bile, or blood microbiome accessions.

use_cases.title: Immunosuppression and molecular graft state
use_cases.clinical_question: How do tacrolimus, cyclosporine, mycophenolate, steroid exposure, and pharmacogenetic context relate to graft molecular injury or rejection states?
use_cases.why_it_matters: Treatment decisions depend on balancing under-immunosuppression, toxicity, infection, and graft injury. This cannot be answered by expression alone.
current_evidence: No drug exposure, pharmacogenetic, or metabolite layer has been processed yet.
This is a required multi-omics extension before clinical monitoring claims are credible.
next_validation: Curate public datasets with immunosuppressant exposure, trough level, or pharmacogenetic metadata.
Link drug metabolism genes, blood/liver expression, and metabolite/protein evidence.
